title: Minecraft Bedrock Camera Preset 1.21.80
description: A camera preset definition for a behavior pack.

preset.description: Identifier for a predefined aim assist preset.

target_mode.description: Mode for selecting aim assist targets.

angle.description: Maximum angle for aim assist targeting.

distance.description: Maximum distance for aim assist targeting.

identifier.description: Namespaced identifier for this camera preset.

inherit_from.description: Another camera preset identifier to inherit properties from.

pos_x.description: Camera position X coordinate in world space.

pos_y.description: Camera position Y coordinate in world space.

pos_z.description: Camera position Z coordinate in world space.

rot_x.description: Starting rotation pitch (vertical angle). Limited to 90 (facing straight down) to -90 (facing straight up).

rot_y.description: Starting rotation yaw (horizontal angle). Rotates 360 degrees around the target parallel to the earth.

rotation_speed.description: By default this is set to 0.0f which is "perfect" tracking and will always keep up with the target no matter the speed.

snap_to_target.description: Whether the camera snaps to the target.

horizontal_rotation_limit.description: Horizontal rotation (yaw) limits as [min, max] in degrees.

vertical_rotation_limit.description: Vertical rotation (pitch) limits as [min, max] in degrees.

continue_targeting.description: Whether to continue targeting when out of range.

tracking_radius.description: Radius within which the camera tracks the target.

view_offset.description: Anchors the camera to a point in the focal view that is not the player. The player will be shifted to the side of this offset.

entity_offset.description: Anchors the camera to a pivot point on the player (0, 0, 0 is the center of the player). Only usable with the follow_orbit preset.

radius.description: Camera distance from the target, measured in blocks.

listener.description: Audio listener type. 'camera' uses the camera position, 'player' uses the player position.

player_effects.description: Whether player effects are enabled for this camera preset.

control_scheme.description: Defines how Minecraft responds to game controllers, touch input on mobile devices, keyboard and mouse combos, and full keyboard controls.
